channel.title: Jornal TRIBUNA DE MACAU EN
channel.description: Jornal TRIBUNA DE MACAU EN RSS

item.title: Smuggling by minors quadrupled in the year
item.description:
Data provided to the TRIBUNA DE MACAU newspaper by the Customs Service reveals that, in the space of a year, smuggling involving minors increased by 352% to 122 cases, this being the highest number since 2019. The body led by Adriano Ho expressed concern about the growing trend, stressing that offenders take advantage of the “weak legal awareness of young people, enticing them with monetary rewards”. Academics interviewed by this newspaper note that in recent years lifestyle and mobility have changed, with border crossings becoming much more frequent. In this sense, and if illicit activities are organized, minors are a target group. At the same time, there may be financial difficulties in families that need to be addressed. They also warn that statistical data may only be showing “the tip of the iceberg”
Catarina Pereira
The Customs Services (SA) have observed a growing trend in the participation of minors in smuggling, they revealed to the TRIBUNA DE MACAU newspaper, adding that, last year, 122 cases of illegal transport involving minors were intercepted. The number represents an increase of around 352% in annual terms – since, in 2024, 27 cases had been registered – and is the highest since at least 2019. According to statistical data provided by the organization led by Adriano Ho to this newspaper, in 2019 only four cases of this kind were recorded, a number that rose to seven a year later, when the first cases of covid-19 were recorded in the territory. In 2021, SA recorded a case of smuggling by minors; the following year there were 12 cases and in 2023 there were five. This year, SA has intercepted a total of nine cases of smuggling involving minors. "The young people involved in these cases were mainly transporting undeclared goods when entering and leaving Macau. Taking this year as an example, the items seized were mainly used electronic products, including cell phones, laptops, tablets and batteries, in a total of 801 units", added the organization. “In recent years, the SA has seen a growing tendency for minors to participate in parallel trade activities, with offenders taking advantage of young people's weak legal awareness, enticing them with monetary rewards to transport goods in and out of Macau, thus avoiding customs inspection”, he notes. The director of the Faculty of Health Sciences at the University of São José (USJ), Jacky Ho, noted that, “surprisingly, compared to 2024, this type of smuggling has increased almost six and a half times”. “Considering the size of Macau and the number of young people nowadays, it is something that society, the Government, parents and families must monitor closely”, he stated, speaking to the TRIBUNA DE MACAU newspaper. From his perspective, as they are minors, “they are practically not subject to any responsibility or consequences”. In the case of minors aged between 16 and 18, they normally receive a fine, while if they are under 16 their property is confiscated. “Smuggling involves a very quick and low-risk financial incentive”, highlighted Jacky Ho. In the same vein, Alexandr Svetlicinii, associate professor at the Faculty of Law of the University of Macau, also considers the increase in the number of cases to be “quite worrying”. “It demonstrates that this activity may be more widespread than authorities or the general public tend to believe,” he said. “The high frequency of passages through border posts, the low probability of being detected and the economic rewards offered by the organizers of smuggling activities may have encouraged young people”, observed the academic. At the same time, Svetlicinii warns that “the increase in the number of detections may suggest not an increase in smuggling activities involving young people, but rather a strengthening of efforts by customs authorities to detect or prosecute these illegal activities”. “In other words, when analyzing detection statistics, we may only be seeing ‘the tip of the iceberg,’” he added.
Lifestyle changes with “open” borders Speaking to this newspaper, social worker Jayson Lou began by recalling that the mobility and lifestyle of Macau citizens have changed in recent years. "More people have moved to Mainland China, the transport of goods from one border to another has become normal. In the mind of students, it is just a way of getting some 'bonus', so they have no feeling of strangeness about it and are not aware that it is something illegal", highlighted the professor at the Faculty of Health Sciences at USJ. Jacky Ho, in turn, recalls that it is “very easy” for minors to cross the border: “Teenagers as young as 12 can cross the border alone. If we consider the age group from 12 to 16 years old, this group will be an easy target, if these activities are organized. They will be attracted by a relatively small amount, which for an adult is small, but for a teenager it is considerable”. "Whoever controls the operations sees this as an opportunity to use young people to smuggle goods across the border. This is a very big warning sign", he considered. Also mentioning that “a large number of young people, including minors, cross the Macau-Zhuhai border posts every day”, Alexandr Svetlicinii adds: “Due to this high frequency of crossings and the technical limitations of customs authorities, it becomes relatively easy for young people to ‘transport items for someone else’ with a low probability of being detected”. Furthermore, “the organizers of smuggling activities can offer a sufficiently attractive remuneration that can be seen by young people as an additional source of income”. Jayson Lou emphasizes this, remembering that the pandemic caused loss of income for several families in the territory, so having “immediate compensation” is “one of the mentalities”. “There are parents who think about how to increase family income in this way and at the same time their children would like to ‘help’. This shows that the employment situation is still not good enough”, he argued. The social worker emphasizes that “the fear of losing money traumatized people”. "During the pandemic, some families didn't lose their jobs, but they didn't have enough work, they saw their salary reduced or they didn't have paid vacations... They lost a lot of savings, they had loans from the bank. Therefore, what they think about smuggling is that it's just a way to gather some more savings to face another challenge, if there is one. And the minors are just thinking about how to help the family", considered Jayson Lou. When Lou was on the front lines in recent years, it was common for there to be a lot of smuggling cases involving elderly people, “but in recent years, it seems like the cases cover all types of families,” he said.
Give another direction to the “entrepreneurial spirit” Alexandr Svetlicinii considers that “one of the measures that can be taken to prevent smuggling activities by young people is to increase their awareness of the negative consequences associated with this illegal activity”. Jacku Ho suggests a different approach: “It could be interesting to cultivate this entrepreneurial spirit and capacity, guiding young people onto the right path”. “If the Macau Government is really committed to helping teenagers develop their individual talents, perhaps we should create a suitable platform, bringing together the right people and training young people so that they have entrepreneurial skills and can earn some money from it”, stressed the director of the Faculty of Health Sciences at USJ. To prevent and stop this scenario, the SA states that they have established a communication mechanism with the Directorate of Education and Youth Development Services. “Whenever the involvement of minors is detected, SA activates this mechanism, allowing the timely intervention of education services, which provide adequate support and advice to young people”, it can be read. Furthermore, the SA emphasize that “in recent years they have intensified dissemination and legal education actions in schools and higher education institutions”, explaining to young students the legal provisions and “the serious consequences of participating in parallel trade activities, guiding them in building correct values ​​and avoiding illicit activities”.

item.title: Boss wants to raise cooperation with Guangdong to “new level”
item.description:
Macau and Guangdong will strengthen bilateral cooperation, according to Sam Hou Fai, during a meeting with Huang Kunming and Meng Fanli, secretary and deputy secretary, respectively, of the Guangdong provincial committee of the Chinese Communist Party. The Chief Executive called on the Guangdong Government to continue to “provide strong support”, pointing to greater integration in terms of rules, innovation, economic development and public services
Sam Hou Fai stated that the Macao SAR and Guangdong province will boost “pragmatic bilateral cooperation in more areas” and “to a new level”. The Chief Executive led a Macao SAR delegation to the city of Guangzhou to meet with Huang Kunming, secretary of the Guangdong provincial committee of the Chinese Communist Party (CCP), and Meng Fanli, governor of Guangdong Province and deputy secretary of the CPC provincial committee. On the occasion, and according to a press release from the Social Communication Office, the leader of the Macao SAR said he hoped that Guangdong Province would “continue to provide strong support, especially on the basis of deeper comprehensive cooperation” between the two parties, and “continuous advances in key areas of collaboration”. Furthermore, it also planned to reinforce the “articulation of rules” and “coordination of mechanisms”, in the areas of scientific and technological innovation, economic development and public services. As part of the development of the Deep Cooperation Zone between Guangdong and Macau in Hengqin, the Chief Executive called for alignment with the “three verifications” proposed by President Xi Jinping, with a view to “creating a series of emblematic and dynamic works and projects”. As examples, Sam Hou Fai mentioned the University City of International Education, the freight terminal at Macau International Airport and the high-speed train section between Guangzhou and Zhuhai, in Hengqin, all described in the 15th Five-Year Plan. At the same time, the synergistic development of industries between Macau and Hengqin, the integration of social services and those related to the lives of the population and infrastructures in the Cooperation Zone will be promoted, said Sam Hou Fai. In turn, Huang Kunming highlighted the “new and important opportunities to boost cooperation between Guangdong and Macau to a higher level, from a new starting point”. The secretary of the provincial committee stated that Guangdong will “fully assume the role of the main force and locomotive” in the development of the Greater Bay Area. Through “more effective” leadership, he promised “new strategic advantages” and “significant progress in this new phase of national development”. In addition to building a “high-quality” and “world-class” Greater Bay Area, both sides stressed the need to implement “the spirit of the two sessions of the National People's Congress and the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference” and to implement the strategic plans of the 15th National Five-Year Plan.
P.M.

item.title: “Macao Water” predicts a 3% increase in water consumption
item.description:
“Macao Water” predicts an increase of around 3% in total water consumption for this year, after having registered a slight decline of 0.7% in 2025. The increase is due to factors such as the gradual occupation of public housing buildings in Zone A and the buildings in Lot P, and through the entry into operation of several hotels in COTAI. The company's executive director, Kuan Sio Peng, said that 2026 will bring “new opportunities and challenges”, one of which is the supply of recycled water. Furthermore, he revealed that the company will organize a forum aimed at Greater Bay Area
VÍTOR REBELO
Last year, water consumption recorded a slight decline of 0.7%, according to “Macao Water”. However, with the gradual occupation of the public housing buildings in Zone A and the buildings in Lot P and with the entry into operation of several hotels in COTAI, an increase of around 3% in the city's total water consumption is expected by 2026. The estimate is from the company's executive director, Kuan Sio Peng. According to him, the Macau Water Supply Society (SAAM) worked “in close coordination with the Government” in the execution of several projects, including the work to expand the Seac Pai Van Dam, “aimed at increasing local freshwater reserves and contingency storage capacity”. In the speech given at the spring lunch offered to the media, the person in charge stated that the new year “will confront SAAM with new opportunities and challenges”, highlighting that the supply of recycled water “is now part of the company's public water supply services”. This March, SAAM began “officially supplying recycled water to the Seac Pai Van Public Housing Complex and the University of Macau, inaugurating a new chapter in the development of unconventional water resources and achieving the objective of circular use of valuable water resources”, he highlighted. In her presentation of the work carried out in 2025 and the projects for 2026, the “Macao Water” executive mentioned that the company also continued to promote the installation of the water supply network and shared underground pipelines in Zone A of the New Landfills. Furthermore, “it took full advantage of the pipelines installed on the three bridges over the sea, together with the Seac Pai Van Water Treatment Plant, to improve the water supply network between the north and south”, he noted. Kuan Sio Peng also recalled the beginning of the second phase of the expansion work at the Pinggang Pumping Station, in Zhuhai, “providing a more stable and reliable raw water supply and thus ensuring greater security in the water supply to Macau”. In terms of inter-regional cooperation, SAAM joined the Guangdong-Hong Kong-Macao Greater Bay Area Water Conservation Sector Alliance last year, with the aim of promoting innovation in the field of water conservation, and also participated, for the first time, as a member of the organizing committee in a symposium promoted by the International Water Association. Furthermore, “as a partner in the construction of a shared water infrastructure for more than 65 years”, “Macao Water” signed, at the end of 2025, a strategic framework agreement for the Deep Cooperation Zone with the “Zhuhai Water Environment Holdings Group”. Focusing on five aspects, the partnership aims to deepen collaboration between Zhuhai and Macau in that Zone, “in order to promote the high-quality development of both Macau and Hengqin”, he stated. As part of the popularization of science and education for water conservation, SAAM organized a range of events, including the Ilha Verde Water Treatment Plant Open Day, trips to water treatment plants, professional technical lectures and study visits to the Government's “Science and Technology Youth Village”, having also published content on social media. Through these initiatives, “Macao Water” aims to “raise greater awareness of the challenges of daily water supply, raise public awareness of the importance of water conservation and cultivate good habits for intelligent and efficient use of water”. In the middle of this year, the company, which celebrated 90 years of existence, will organize the “7th Water Forum of the Guangdong-Hong Kong-Macao Greater Bay Area and the 16th Water Supply Sector Congress of Shenzhen, Hong Kong, Zhuhai and Macau”, which will be dedicated to the theme “Water Nourishes the Greater Bay Area, Intelligence Guides the Future”, with the event, for the first time, being held in Hengqin. Kuan Sio Peng hopes that “this major industry event will bring together all the wisdom of the water sector in the Greater Bay Area, facilitate the joint exploration of synergies and innovation in the development of the water sector by operators, and further promote the collaboration and integrated development of Hengqin and Macau”.

item.title: Legal gaming reforms aligned with China’s “priorities”
item.description:
REUTERS/Bobby Yip/File Photo
The dismantling of the VIP gambling promoter system in Macau, once responsible for more than 70% of casino revenue, marked a decisive change in China's governance priorities, according to a study by the University of Macau. The authors, João Ilhão Moreira and Zhou Yudi, concluded that reforms introduced between 2022 and 2024 by the Macau Government aligned the gaming industry with Beijing's broader goals of greater capital controls, enhanced transparency and law-based governance. The study states that the end of these VIP promoters (known as “junkets”) reflected a strategic alignment with “mainland China objectives, including greater capital control”, strengthening financial transparency and law-based governance. The work highlights that the “junkets” model, which served high-level clients and facilitated opaque flows of capital from Mainland China, has become increasingly incompatible with the application of anti-money laundering rules and ongoing modernization. “Although it has been integral to Macau’s growth, the junket system has come into increasing conflict with the enforcement of anti-money laundering rules and the modernization of the sector,” the authors wrote. The study traces the evolution of the industry since the transfer of sovereignty in 1999, noting that junkets were tolerated for decades due to their contribution to the game's gross revenue. In 2011, Macau's gaming revenue reached 269.1 billion patacas, five times the revenue of Las Vegas, and with VIP rooms contributing more than 70% of this revenue. The arrest and conviction, in 2021, of Alvin Chau, leader of the largest group of “junkets” in the territory, the Suncity Group, symbolized the end of an era and accelerated the transition to a gaming market model centered on occasional players and integrated tourism. Chau was later sentenced to 18 years in prison, having been found guilty of more than 100 crimes, including leadership of a criminal association, fraud and illegal gambling exploitation, involving bets exceeding HK$823.7 billion. According to researchers, the “junket” system evolved into a powerful financial network, which extended far beyond the gaming rooms. The operators were not only intermediaries for VIP clients, but also facilitators of large opaque capital flows between Mainland China and Macau. The authors pointed out that VIP lounges and junkets “created networks of unregulated capital flows”, establishing parallel channels outside China's official banking system. These networks have become problematic as Beijing has tightened capital controls and demanded greater transparency. The study highlights that, although tolerated for years, junkets depended on side bets and discreet transfers of funds, practices that ended up colliding with China's modernization objectives. In 2022, the Macao SAR Government advanced the first major review of the Gaming Law in more than two decades. The reform introduced profound changes: it redefined the role of concessionaires and gambling promoters, practically dismantled the “junkets” system and reinforced control and transparency mechanisms. The new legal framework began to favor the diversification of the industry, with a greater focus on the mass market and integrated tourism, to the detriment of VIP lounges, dominated by “junkets”. Furthermore, it aligned Macau with China's national priorities, namely strengthening capital control, preventing money laundering and integrating the MSAR into the national economic development strategy. “Legal and regulatory measures introduced between 2022 and 2024 were designed to align Macau’s gaming industry with China’s priorities,” the study concludes.
JTM with Lusa

item.title: Trump plans to postpone visit to China for a month due to war
item.description:
North American President Donald Trump has declared that he plans to postpone his trip to China, scheduled for the end of March to meet with his counterpart Xi Jinping, for approximately a month due to the war with Iran. "Because of the conflict, I want to be here. I feel like I need to be here. That's why we requested a postponement for about a month, and I'm looking forward to being with them. We have a great relationship," the American leader told the press. White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt had previously indicated on Monday that Trump's trip to Beijing, initially scheduled for the period between March 31 and April 3, could be postponed because the President is focused on the offensive in Iran. Over the weekend, in an interview with the “Financial Times”, Trump had already suggested that he could postpone the visit until he knows Beijing's position on its readiness to help guarantee the security of the Strait of Hormuz, an important oil transport route that was blocked by Iran in retaliation for US airstrikes. However, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, who met with Chinese representatives in Paris on Monday as part of trade negotiations between the two countries, told journalists that a possible postponement of the trip was not due to disagreements over the situation in the Strait of Hormuz. “A postponement would not be the result of a failure to comply with a request from the President,” said Bessent. Yesterday, the Chinese Government classified as “erroneous” the news published by North American authorities and the media that suggested a possible postponement of Trump's visit to China due to issues related to the escort of ships in the Strait of Hormuz. The spokesperson for the Chinese Ministry of Foreign Affairs, Lin Jian, declared at a press conference that both powers “maintain communication regarding Trump's visit” to China, without providing further details. On Monday, China once again called for an end to the escalation in the Strait of Hormuz and the suspension of military actions in the region, without clarifying whether it would participate in the international coalition to escort ships proposed by Trump. If it comes to fruition, this will be Trump's second visit to the Asian giant, after the trip made in 2017 during his first term. Trump and Xi last met in October last year, in South Korea. Preparations for the trip coincide with a context of commercial uncertainty, after the US Supreme Court recently limited part of the legal framework used by the Trump administration to impose widespread tariffs on China.
JTM with international agencies

item.title: Portuguese Environment Agency once again represented at MIECF
item.description:
The construction of an ecological civilization, waste management, green finance and regional cooperation in environmental protection will be the focus of the four sessions of the “Green Forum” integrated into the MIECF. According to the organization, the president of the Board of Directors of the Portuguese Environment Agency, José Carlos Pimenta Machado, will once again be present as a speaker
This year's Macau International Environmental Cooperation Forum and Exhibition (MIECF) will have four thematic sessions, which will focus on building an ecological civilization, the waste management system, green finance and regional cooperation in the field of environmental protection. The sessions will feature renowned local experts and academics from Mainland China, Hong Kong, other Southeast Asian countries and Portuguese-speaking countries, including José Carlos Pimenta Machado, president of the Board of Directors of the Portuguese Environment Agency, who will once again participate in the event, according to the organization. On the afternoon of March 27th, “Session 4 of the Green Forum: Building Zero Waste Cities through Innovative Thinking” will take place in the “Sicily” Conference Room at the Venetian. Throughout the session, guests will discuss “how to reformulate the solid waste management system through innovative thinking”, with an emphasis on strategies and practical solutions, in order to promote the transformation of the “end-of-life treatment” of waste. Topics to be discussed include “Portugal’s Path to Low-Carbon and Zero-Waste Cities: Innovation, Public Policies and Replicable Municipal Solutions”, “Innovative Practices that are Transforming Beijing into a Zero-Waste City”, “Food Waste and Green@Community” and “Path and Perspectives for Building High-Quality, Zero-Waste Cities in the Greater Bay Area”, among others. Chaired by Wong Seng Fat, deputy director of Cheng Yu Tung College at the University of Macau, the session will have simultaneous translation, “providing participants with an international platform for exchanging information on environmental protection without barriers”. Additionally, the organization said it will continue to request professional certification for the forums and will continue to issue certificates of participation. According to a statement, “the amount of waste produced annually in cities around the world is enormous, and if it is not treated and reused properly, this could put pressure on the urban environment and the treatment of solid waste.” In this sense, it defends “a new future of sustainable, ecological development with low carbon emissions”, through the sharing of experiences and practical results in promoting the construction of cities with zero waste.

item.title: Crisis compromises the continuity of FC Porto de Macau
item.description:
The subsidiary of Futebol Clube do Porto in Macau is going through a financial and operational crisis that could jeopardize its continuity, according to a letter sent to members by management. In the letter, the director of Casa do Futebol Clube do Porto de Macau – China, Diana Massada, pointed out that the club is facing “serious financial and operational difficulties”, which recently culminated in an eviction process from the headquarters and the existence of several unresolved debts. Massada recalls that, over eight years, the current management dedicated time and personal resources that helped to overcome specific difficulties and allow the club to continue to exist, but admits that “the situation has become particularly difficult” in the last year and a half. Among the factors highlighted are the lack of regular payment of dues - only around 10 out of more than 100 members have their dues up to date -, the absence of sponsors, structural problems in the leased headquarters and difficulties in operating the entity's canteen, which should be one of the main sources of revenue. The president also highlights that the active participation of members has been limited, which has limited the ability to develop projects and overcome demanding moments. In the letter, the management appeals for the support of members to regularize quotas, identify sponsors and collaborate on projects such as the Football School or youth volleyball. One of the immediate needs mentioned is to remove and sell assets from the old headquarters to settle small debts. “The club is going through a difficult time, but continues to represent many years of dedication, coexistence, sports practice and community spirit”, wrote Diana Massada, appealing to the “collective will” of the members to guarantee the continuity of the House of FC Porto de Macau. FC Porto de Macau was founded in 2006 and quickly ascended to the I Division of football in the territory, where it came second. The last time the club was present in Macau's first division was in 2012, with financial problems pushing the team down to the last division. FC Porto de Macau's senior eleven football team fell from the third to the fourth division last season, two seasons after winning promotion, following 10 defeats, one draw and zero wins. The big three of Portuguese football have counterpart clubs in the Macao SAR, with Casa do Benfica de Macau – holder of six championships won – in the territory's first league, and Sporting de Macau currently in the second division.
JTM with Lusa

item.title: History and iconography in female writing and funerary images
item.description: The session “Itinerarium Egeriae and Catacombs frescoes: History and Iconography in Feminine Writing and Funerary Images” will take place today at the Rui Cunha Foundation, as part of the cycle of Doctoral Lectures in the City. The organization takes advantage of the presence in the territory of specialist Sílvia Siqueira, associate professor at the State University of Ceará, Brazil, at the invitation of the University of São José (USJ). The session, which starts at 6:30 pm, will be moderated by professor Adérito Fernandes-Marcos, director of the USJ Doctoral School, and who is also coordinator of this series of lectures. Sílvia Siqueira will discuss Egéria's itinerary and will do a “comparative analysis between the written text, especially the biblical passages and some images present in catacombs and funeral monuments of women, created between the 3rd century and the 4th century AD.” The speaker considers that female figures, in early Christian art, played significant roles in the transmission of faith. "Egeria, a devout Christian pilgrim, left a record of her pilgrimage trip to the Holy Land. It is a rare and precious document, as it is one of the few records written in antiquity by a woman", says the teacher. According to Sílvia Siqueira, Egéria “describes not only the journey, but also narrates in detail the experience of the pilgrimage and describes the Christian liturgical practice in Jerusalem”. The coordinator of the Research Group “Narratives, Experiences and Spaces in the ancient and medieval world”, and of the Laboratory for Intercultural Studies on East and West in São Paulo, adds that “the text exudes the landscape and imagery of a trip to the Holy Land, and describes the stimulating experience of crossing those sacred lands”. “For her, much more than an immersion in her spirituality, it is also a clear ‘spatial otherness’, the stimulating encounter with ‘other’ individuals and societies.” Apparently very simple, whether in the narrative aspect or in the symbolic aspect, both this document and the messages present in the funerary images have a pedagogical potential, capable of elucidating, teaching, transporting beliefs and revealing traces of the collective mentality through time, it can be read. For example, "Egéria talks very little about herself, but informs her interlocutors about the places visited, the lush landscape and the spiritual experience. Even though it is a document written by a fervent pilgrim, there is not much information about the practical life of the devout Christians." Sílvia Siqueira has a degree in Social Sciences, a master's degree and a PhD in History. He also completed a post-doctoral internship at the Università degli Studi Roma Tre, in Italy. She is dedicated to the study of Christianity in Late Antiquity, focusing on the actions of women in the religious, social and cultural spheres. Their works give visibility and affirm the presence and uniqueness of women, and their leading role in the construction of collective historical memory. Currently, he is dedicated to studying the Christian East, with emphasis on Africa and its cultural and religious connections. The lecture will be held in Portuguese and is free to attend.

item.title: AL grasped the spirit of the “Two Sessions”
item.description: The Legislative Assembly (AL) promoted a session to convey the spirit of the “Two Sessions” corresponding to the Fourth Session of the 14th National People's Assembly (ANP) and the 14th National Committee of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference (CPPCC). During the meeting, the president of the AL, André Cheong, highlighted that deputies must put into practice, “with concrete actions”, the principle of “predominance of executive power”, in order to ensure that “there are no gaps in support for the Government, and there is no usurpation in monitoring the Government”. André Cheong hopes that deputies will present “constructive opinions”, aiming for greater “scientificity and viability” of the Macao SAR's 3rd Five-Year Plan. Meanwhile, Chui Sai Peng, deputy head of the Macau delegation to the ANP, highlighted that, with the creation of specific chapters for Hong Kong and Macau, the country's 15th Five-Year Plan expressly defines the need to “promote the prosperity and lasting stability of the two regions” and to “support Hong Kong and Macau in consolidating and increasing their competitive advantages”. In turn, Ho Ion Sang, vice-president of the AL and member of the Standing Committee of the National Committee of the CPPCC considered that Macau must implement the spirit of the “Two Sessions” in four aspects, focusing on national security and deepening administrative reform; in the country's development as the only way to promote adequate diversification of the economy; in taking advantage of the unique advantages of the Macao SAR as a bridge to the world; and the well-being of the population.

item.title: AMCM urged the sector to take advantage of the platform’s “advantages”
item.description:
The Macau Monetary Authority (AMCM) brought together around 160 representatives from the Macao SAR and Guangdong, as part of the “Presentation session on the advantages and development opportunities of the Macau investment funds sector”. Participants included members of public utilities, associations, investment fund management companies, banks, securities groups and technology companies. During the session, in the Deep Cooperation Zone between Guangdong and Macau in Hengqin, Vong Sin Man, chairman of the Board of Directors of AMCM, said he hopes “that the sector can take advantage of the advantages of the Macau platform to explore international and Portuguese-speaking Countries” (PLP) markets, as well as offering “more diversified options” for asset allocation to national and foreign investors. In summary, Vong stated that the Macao SAR must improve its role as a "Financial Services Platform between China and the PLP. Furthermore, he said that the entry into force of the "Investment Fund Law" this year made it possible to promote "convergence and interconnection of supervision between the MSAR's fund market and foreign markets", as well as "a more favorable business environment." Fu Yongge, member of the Hengqin Working Committee of the Provincial Committee of Guangdong of the Chinese Communist Party (CCP), highlighted the “huge potential and space for coordinated development in the modern financial sector between Hengqin and Macau”. For the deputy head of the executive committee of Hengqin, the new law “enables the promotion of interconnection and interconnection” between the international and Macau funds markets. Macau Funds, with the support of the Trade and Investment Promotion Institute.
P.M.

item.title: New round of ticket sales for ITTF cups
item.description: The second round of ticket sales for the Men's and Women's World Cups of the International Table Tennis Federation (ITTF) begins today, at 12 p.m., according to an announcement from the Sports Institute (ID), after the first phase of ticket sales received “an overwhelming response”. Interested parties will be able to purchase tickets through the Galaxy Online Ticket Office, the Mpay and “Damai” platforms or “MacauTicket”. Prices vary between MOP90 and MOP1,720, depending on the place and session. Each person can purchase a maximum of four tickets per transaction, and an adult with a ticket may bring a child at no additional cost, as long as the child is less than 1.2 meters tall and does not occupy any seat. Macau students, elderly residents and Disability Assessment Registration Card holders can enjoy a 20% discount when purchasing tickets through “MacauTicket” for sessions S1-S10, which will take place from March 30th to April 3rd. Each person can only purchase one discounted ticket, the number is limited. The organizing entities appeal to the public to use authorized sales channels. Furthermore, the ID warns that “the resale of tickets at inflated prices is prohibited”. Organized by Galaxy, ID and the Macau General Ping-Pong Association, the ITTF World Cups will take place at the Galaxy Arena, between March 30th and April 5th.

item.title: Sam Hou Fai visits Portugal from April 17th
item.description: The Macao SAR Government confirmed yesterday that the Chief Executive, Sam Hou Fai, will visit Portugal and Spain between the 17th and 23rd of April. This is Sam Hou Fai's first official visit abroad since taking office in December 2024. The visit program has not yet been revealed. Remember that the trip was scheduled for the first half of last year, but ended up being postponed due to the change of Government in Portugal. It was later rescheduled for September, but postponed again. When he took office, Sam Hou Fai highlighted as a priority the promotion of financial and commercial services between China and Hispanic countries, as a complement to the role of a Sino-Lusophone platform assigned by the Central Government to Macau. The last time a Macao SAR Chief Executive went to Portugal was in 2023, on what was former leader Ho Iat Seng's first trip abroad, after three years of the pandemic.

item.title: TSI INCREASED KONG CHI'S PRISON SENTENCE TO 24 YEARS
item.description: The Court of Second Instance (TSI) yesterday handed down its ruling on the second phase of the case relating to former deputy prosecutor Kong Chi. The collective court decided to find the first defendant, Kong Chi, guilty of crimes of passive corruption for an unlawful act, malfeasance, abuse of power, association or secret society. In a legal coup, Kong Chi was sentenced to a single sentence of 24 years in prison. This sentence represents an increase in the 21-year sentence imposed in the first phase of the process. The second defendant, lawyer Ho Kam Meng, and the third defendant, businesswoman Choi Sao Ieng, were also found guilty of multiple crimes, including corruption and association or secret society, and were sentenced to 15 years and 16 years and nine months in prison, respectively. The second phase of the trial is based on the accusation that Kong Chi, in collusion with Ho Kam Meng and Choi Sao Ieng, among others, helped to handle several criminal cases, including cases of hiring illegal workers, drugs and falsifying documents, through a “case separation” method over a long period, and received illicit benefits in return. When reading the ruling, and according to the newspaper “Ou Mun”, Choi Mou Pan, president of the TSI, stated that Kong Chi took advantage of his duties as deputy prosecutor to close cases, thus exempting the people involved from accusations and receiving benefits from these individuals. According to him, some cases also indicated a division of tasks between Kong Chi and Ho Kam Meng or Choi Sao Ieng, with Kong using his position as a public servant to carry out acts that contradict his duties, promoting the dismissal of cases. Choi Mou Pan noted that Kong Chi's illicit acts had a profound impact on Macau. As for Ho Kam Meng, he pointed out that, despite having dedicated himself to causes of social interest, in this case, the criminal intention is serious. The fourth defendant, lawyer Kuan Hoi Lon, was acquitted, as the collective court considers that her conduct was limited to the practice of law, and no evidence was found of her direct participation in the “separation of processes” or sharing of benefits. The first three defendants were present at the reading of the sentence, while Kuan Hoi Lon was absent.

item.title: “One Battle After Another” dominated the Oscars
item.description:
EPA/CHRIS TORRES
The Oscar for Best Film went to “One Battle After Another”, by Paul Thomas Anderson, at the 98th edition of the US Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences Awards, held in Los Angeles. Paul Thomas Anderson's dramatic comedy thus proved to be the winner of this edition, winning six Oscars out of the 13 nominations with which it had arrived at the ceremony, the majority in the main categories (Best Film, Director, Adapted Screenplay, Supporting Actor, Editing and Casting), while “Sinners”, which gathered a record number of 16 nominations, obtained four (Best Actor, Original Screenplay, Cinematography and Soundtrack). “One Battle After Another” follows the story of Bob Ferguson, played by Leonardo DiCaprio, a former left-wing revolutionary who has to save his daughter from the clutches of a white supremacist (Sean Penn). In his acceptance speech for the award for best adapted screenplay, Anderson dedicated the victory to his children, stating that he wrote the film as an apology to them for the “confusion” left by previous generations. According to the director, “One Battle After Another” reflects the desire to recover “common sense and decency”. “Our film obviously has a lot of parallels to what’s happening in the news every day,” he said. “It reflects what is happening in our world,” said the filmmaker. The other eight candidates for Best Picture were “Bugonia” by Yorgos Lanthimos, “F1” by Joseph Kosinski, “Frankenstein” by Guillermo del Toro, “Hamnet” by Chloé Zhao, “Marty Supreme” by Josh Safdie, “The Secret Agent” by Kléber Mendonça Filho, “Sentimental Value” by Joachim Trier, “Sinners” by Ryan Coogler and “Train Dreams” by Clint Bentley. In this edition of the Oscars, “Frankenstein” occupied third place, with three Oscars (Best Production Design, Costume and Characterization) and “KPop Demon Hunters”, fourth, with two Oscars (Best Animated Film and Song). Of the nominees for Best Film, three productions won an Oscar: “Hamnet”, which started with eight nominations, won Best Actress (Jessie Buckley), “Sentimental Value”, with nine nominations, won Best International Film, and “F1”, nominated for four Oscars, most in technical categories, won Best Sound. Without any prize were “Marty Supreme”, nominated for nine categories, “Bugonia”, for four, and “Train Dreams”, for three. Expectations were also disappointed for the Brazilian production “The Secret Agent”, which had four nominations: Best Film, Best Actor (Wagner Moura), Best International Film and Best Casting. This year, the work of casting directors was recognized for the first time, with the Oscar being awarded to Cassandra Kulukundis, for the casting of “One Battle After Another”, and, for the first time, a woman was distinguished in photography direction: Autumn Durald Arkapaw, for her work on “Sinners”. Among the 'cases' to highlight in this edition of the Oscars, there is also the "tie" in the category of Best Live Action Short Film, which had two winners: "The Singers", by Sam A. Davis and Jack Piatt, and "Two People Exchanging Saliva", by Alexandre Singh and Natalie Musteata. This is not unprecedented at the Oscars, although unlikely, as victory results from a vote by Academy members. According to Hollywood awards statistics, the “tie” has already happened on six occasions: in 1932, with actors Fredric March and Wallace Beery; in 1950, with the short documentary films “A Chance to Live” and “So Much for So Little”; in 1969, with actresses Barbra Streisand and Katharine Hepburn; in 1987, with the documentaries “Artie Shaw: Time Is All You’ve Got” and “Down and Out in America”; in 1995, also in the category of Best Live Action Short Film, which distinguished “Franz Kafka’s It’s a Wonderful Life” and “Trevor”; and in 2013, with the Sound of “Zero Dark Thirty” and “Skyfall”. The ceremony took place at the Dolby Theater in Los Angeles, with a presentation by comedian Connan O’Brian.
Kimmel criticizes Trump and Melania "Mr. Nobody Against Putin" and "All the Empty Rooms" won the documentary film Oscars, announced by Jimmy Kimmel. The comedian praised reality cinema, without artifice or censorship, pointing to the US Presidency, ironizing the fact that “the [president's] wife's film”, which shows “choosing shoes in the White House”, was not nominated, in an allusion to “Melania”. “Is he going to be mad that his wife wasn’t nominated for this?” he asked, without mentioning Trump. Kimmel also contrasted the nature of documentary cinema with “North Korea… and CBS”. In 2025, the television station admitted paying compensation to Trump, following a mission from the 60 Minutes program, and last month it did not broadcast an interview with Democratic candidate James Talarico, in the Texas Senate primaries, recorded for Stephen Colbert's “Late Show”. In September, ABC temporarily removed “Jimmy Kimmel Live!” off the air, after the comedian said that Republicans were trying to take political advantage of the death of conservative 'influencer' Charles Kirk.
JTM with Lusa and international agencies
